features.name: Storage Classes
features.description: Offers Standard (active), Nearline (accessed < once/month), Coldline (< once/quarter), and Archive (long-term data retention).

features.name: Lifecycle Management
features.description: Rules to automatically delete or transition objects to cheaper storage classes based on age, creation date, or version count.

features.name: Object Versioning
features.description: Protects against accidental deletion by keeping history of object edits, allowing recovery of older object versions.

features.name: Signed URLs
features.description: Provides temporary, secure access to private storage objects without requiring Google credentials.

iamRoles.description: Full control over buckets, ACLs, and objects in Cloud Storage.
Full control over objects (create, read, delete), but cannot modify bucket metadata.
Read-only access to objects and their metadata.

cliExamples.description: Upload a directory to a Coldline bucket with multithreaded parallel upload
Generate a signed URL for reading a file that expires in 15 minutes

design: Cloud Storage is a globally unified, highly durable, serverless object storage system.
Storage classes determine performance and cost: Standard, Nearline (30d min), Coldline (90d min), and Archive (365d min).
Object names are flat keys; folders are simulated prefix listings (e.g. gs://bucket/folder/file.txt).
Object versioning helps recover from accidental overwrites, keeping active and noncurrent object states.
Dual-region storage replicates data synchronously across two distinct regions, offering 99.999% SLA.
Bucket Lock enforces WORM (Write Once Read Many) policies, making objects immutable for regulatory compliance.
Signed URLs grant time-limited access to specific objects using the signatures of a service account.
Storage Transfer Service transfers petabytes of data from other clouds (S3), HTTP endpoints, or on-premises.
security: Access control can be Uniform (bucket-level IAM only) or Fine-grained (using ACLs on individual objects).
Uniform bucket-level access is highly recommended for security compliance and ease of auditing.
All data in Cloud Storage is encrypted at rest by default; encryption can be CMEK or Customer-Supplied (CSEK).
Public access prevention (PAP) can be enabled on the bucket to prevent accidental exposure of sensitive data.
Data in transit is secured using HTTPS/TLS; access control supports VPC Service Controls security perimeters.
Do not use service account keys directly on clients; generate signed URLs or use Workload Identity.
Object lifecycle actions can delete noncurrent versions to enforce data retention compliance policies.
Audit logs track data access (Object Read, Object Write) and admin operations inside the bucket.
reliability: Cloud Storage offers 99.999999999% (11 9s) annual durability for all storage classes.
Multi-regional and dual-regional configurations replicate data across geographically separated datacenters.
Automatic geo-replication protects data from catastrophic regional disasters and data center failures.
Dual-region buckets have a recovery time objective (RTO) of zero during regional outages.
Standard storage has a 99.9% availability SLA, while Nearline/Coldline is 99.0% and Archive is 99.0%.
Strong global consistency ensures that after a write, updates are immediately visible to subsequent read requests.
Resume aborted uploads automatically using resumable upload sessions for large files (files up to 5TB).
Validate data integrity during transfers automatically using MD5/CRC32C hash checksum checks.
cost: Billed based on storage volume (GB/month), network egress, and operations (Class A: writes, Class B: reads).
Coldline and Archive classes have lower storage fees but higher access costs and minimum storage durations.
Archive storage costs as low as $0.0012 per GB/month, ideal for data kept for compliance purposes.
Retrieving data from Nearline, Coldline, or Archive incurs a retrieval fee per GB accessed.
Lifecycle management rules should automatically downgrade Standard data to Coldline/Archive over time.
Enable lifecycle rules to clean up incomplete multipart uploads after a specified number of days.
Transferring data between Cloud Storage buckets in the same region is free, but cross-region transfer incurs egress fees.
Reduce operational costs by combining many small files into larger archives before uploading.
